name: Folic acid and vitamin B12 reduced micronucleus frequency in hemodialysis patients
summary: The clearest human intervention biomarker signal is a small high-risk dialysis study showing reduced lymphocyte micronuclei after folate and B12 supplementation.
statement: In hemodialysis patients, folic acid and vitamin B12 supplementation reduced peripheral blood lymphocyte micronucleus frequency, a chromosomal-aberration biomarker.
time_horizon: Prospective sampling before and after supplementation
quantitative_note: The abstract reports reduced micronucleus frequency, more markedly with combined folic acid and vitamin B12 supplementation, alongside more efficient homocysteine lowering.
caveats: The study was small and in end-stage renal failure, not a general aging-prevention population.
Micronucleus frequency is a biomarker and does not establish functional, disease, or lifespan benefit.
This one-carbon metabolism signal is adjacent to chromosome stability but not direct spindle-checkpoint enhancement.
